feed.title: Post-Quantum BIP-86 Recovery via zk-STARK Proof of BIP-32 Seed Knowledge

entry.title: Post-Quantum BIP-86 Recovery via zk-STARK Proof of BIP-32 Seed Knowledge
entry.summary:
The recent discussions on the Bitcoin Development Mailing List have delved into the potential application of zk-STARK proofs to enhance the security and privacy of Bitcoin transactions, particularly concerning BIP-32 derived keys and the use of silent payment protocols like BIP-352. The conversation emphasizes the feasibility of using a single zk-STARK proof to authenticate the knowledge of an xpriv key which derives from a specific BIP-32 path. This approach would enable verifiers to easily derive private keys needed for transaction verification, making it possible to cover every UTXO in a silent payment wallet.
Furthermore, there's an exploration of extending these techniques beyond BIP-32 to cover P2PKH and P2WPKH outputs that commit to an unrevealed secret. This involves proving the correctness of HASH160(k·G) without revealing the private key or the pubkey, enhancing the recovery of a broader set of funds while maintaining security integrity. Despite the complexity of securely implementing such proofs, especially given the vulnerability of exposed pubkeys to quantum attacks, the discussion highlights the innovative use of cryptographic proofs to secure Bitcoin transactions against advanced threats.
On a related note, the integration of BIP-352 with silent payments has been tested, showing how the receiver can reconstruct the output using private scan keys, public spend keys, and public transaction data. However, the current proof-of-concept does not fully bind the proof to specific transactions, raising concerns about the potential for replay attacks. Addressing this issue would require further refinement of the zk-STARK proof mechanism to ensure it is tightly coupled with the spending transaction and the inputs being spent.
In terms of practical implementation, one contributor detailed their experiments with optimizing zk-STARK proof generation using GPU acceleration, significantly reducing proving times. These efforts underscore the ongoing advancements in cryptographic proof techniques and their potential to bolster the privacy and security frameworks of Bitcoin transactions. The contributor also shared relevant documentation and resources, which provide deeper insights into the technical underpinnings and outcomes of their experimental approaches ([reduced proof variants documentation](https://github.com/Roasbeef/bip32-pq-zkp/blob/main/docs/reduced-variants.md), [detailed README](https://github.com/Roasbeef/bip32-pq-zkp/blob/1c89fdb398180a2b3eff7761b7f4b233d455c6c9/README.mdreduced-proof-variants), [claims documentation](https://github.com/Roasbeef/bip32-pq-zkp/blob/438c548ca9b49d83ef4019974a5171f5e06fa840/docs/claim.mdreduced-variant-claims)).
Overall, these discussions illuminate the complexities and innovations at the intersection of cryptography and blockchain technology, highlighting both the challenges and the promising solutions that lie ahead in securing digital assets against emerging threats.
